description_long: This is a tactical tomahawk-style axe with a robust, black metallic construction. The head features a dual-purpose design: one side is a sharp, curved blade with a slightly beveled edge, while the opposite side has a pointed spike for piercing or prying. The blade is attached to a straight, cylindrical handle that is textured with deep grooves for enhanced grip and control. The handle appears to be made of metal or reinforced polymer, providing both durability and shock absorption. The axe head is secured with visible bolts or rivets, suggesting a modern, modular assembly. The overall design is utilitarian and aggressive, suitable for urban rescue, breaching, or utility tasks.
description: A black tactical tomahawk axe with a sharp blade, pointed spike, and a grooved, cylindrical handle for secure grip. The head is bolted to the handle, giving it a modern, rugged appearance.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from the front, clearly displaying the curved blade on the left and the pointed spike on the right, with the grooved handle visible below.
description_view_1: A side profile of the axe, showing the thinness of the blade and spike, and the cylindrical handle aligned vertically.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the flat back of the blade and spike, with the handle's grip texture visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile, mirroring view 1, again showing the slim profile of the head and the straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle with deep grooves for grip, likely made of metal or reinforced polymer, non-curved and robust.
attribute_blade_design: Dual-purpose head with a curved, single-edged blade and a pointed spike opposite; blade has a beveled edge and is bolted to the handle.
attribute_mounting_style: Modern bolted mounting with visible fasteners securing the head to the handle.
